claimText: CSS-IDWH is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme administered by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC).
evidenceExcerpt: Centrally Sponsored Scheme under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC).
examUse: Statement-based question on the classification and nodal ministry of the scheme.
distractorHooks: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare

claimText: The CSS-IDWH scheme provides financial assistance for wildlife conservation both inside and outside Protected Areas.
evidenceExcerpt: Funds Protected Areas, wildlife outside PAs, and critically endangered species recovery programmes.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the scope of the scheme's funding.
distractorHooks: Funding is strictly limited to National Parks and Wildlife Sanctuaries
Does not cover wildlife outside Protected Areas

claimText: The Species Recovery Programme under CSS-IDWH currently includes 22 species, such as the Snow Leopard, Asiatic Lion, and Gangetic River Dolphin.
evidenceExcerpt: 22 species currently included, such as Snow Leopard, Asiatic Lion, and Gangetic River Dolphin.
examUse: Statement-based or correct-pair question on the species covered under the recovery programme.
distractorHooks: Excludes aquatic species like the Gangetic River Dolphin

claimText: The Gharial and Sloth Bear have been recommended for inclusion in the Species Recovery Programme under CSS-IDWH.
evidenceExcerpt: Gharial and Sloth Bear recommended for inclusion in the Species Recovery Programme.
examUse: Current affairs or statement-based question on recent recommendations for the recovery programme.
distractorHooks: Cheetah and African Elephant recommended for inclusion
Gharial and Sloth Bear were removed from the programme

claimText: Project Tiger and Project Elephant are key components of the CSS-Integrated Development of Wildlife Habitats Scheme (CSS-IDWH).
evidenceExcerpt: Key components include Project Tiger and Project Elephant.
examUse: Statement-based question on the sub-components of the CSS-IDWH scheme.
distractorHooks: Project Tiger is an independent Central Sector Scheme outside CSS-IDWH
Project Elephant is managed by a separate international body
